name: CoinEx Crypto Exchange API
what_it_does: Hong Kong-based cryptocurrency exchange with REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and developers to access spot trading, perpetual futures, margin trading, market data, and automated market maker (AMM) pools, with HMAC-SHA256 authentication and comprehensive API documentation for algorithmic trading.
use_cases: Executing spot trades via CoinEx API for cryptocurrency algorithmic trading strategies
Managing perpetual futures positions via CoinEx API for derivatives trading bots
Accessing market data via CoinEx API for price feeds and order book depth analysis
Implementing AMM liquidity provision via CoinEx API for automated market making
Streaming real-time data via CoinEx WebSocket for low-latency trading applications
not_for: Institutional custody solutions (use Fireblocks for institutional)
Regulated US derivatives trading (use Coinbase Advanced Trade or Kraken)
best_when: A developer needs access to perpetual futures and AMM features alongside spot trading, with better documentation than many mid-tier exchanges and no US restriction concerns.
avoid_when: You need US compliance, top-tier liquidity, or institutional-grade custody.

auth.notes: HMAC-SHA256 signature. Headers: X-COINEX-KEY, X-COINEX-SIGN, X-COINEX-TIMESTAMP. Timestamp: milliseconds since epoch (within 60 seconds of server time). Signature: HMAC-SHA256 of method + path + timestamp + body (JSON string). API v2 at api.coinex.com/v2/. WebSocket: wss://socket.coinex.com/v2/. API v1 deprecated — use v2. Two API key types: spot/margin keys and futures keys.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; spot trading: 0.2% maker/taker
pricing.paid_tiers: VIP tiers based on 30-day volume; CET token for fee discounts
pricing.notes: Hong Kong-based company. Founded 2017 by ViaBTC Group (ViaBTC mining pool). ~700+ trading pairs. Products: Spot, Margin, Perpetual futures, AMM pools. CET token: native exchange token for fee discounts. API v2 launched 2023 — improved documentation and WebSocket protocol. Separate API keys for spot vs futures (important for permission management). AMM: automated market maker for liquidity provision. US-restricted. KYC levels: unverified (limited), standard, advanced. Well-documented API relative to peers.

performance.rate_limits: Spot: 100 req/sec; Futures: 50 req/sec; varies by endpoint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: X-COINEX-KEY/SIGN/TIMESTAMP headers. HMAC-SHA256 with body included. API v1 DEPRECATED (use v2). Separate keys for spot vs futures. US-restricted. Hong Kong company. AMM pools. 60-second timestamp window.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Order placement: use client_id for idempotent order tracking. Market data reads idempotent. AMM operations: check state before retrying.
known_agent_gotchas: API v1 is DEPRECATED — migrate to v2 (api.coinex.com/v2/); v1 may be removed
Separate API keys for spot/margin vs perpetual futures — use correct key for each market type
HMAC signature: method + path + timestamp + body — body must be raw JSON string (not sorted)
X-COINEX-TIMESTAMP within 60 seconds of server time — clock sync critical
US-restricted exchange — US IP addresses may be blocked
AMM pool operations: separate endpoints from standard trading — different rate limits apply

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 with timestamp — replay attack window 60 seconds. Hong Kong exchange: outside EU/US regulatory jurisdictions. Separate spot vs futures API keys — good permission segmentation. KYC levels: unverified account has trading limits. No OAuth2. API key IP whitelist recommended for production. GDPR: limited applicability from HK; verify for EU users.
